title: angelica archangelica subsp. norwegica
intro: Angelica archangelica subsp. norwegica, also known as Norwegian angelica, is a subspecies of the angelica plant, a biennial or perennial plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to Europe. The medicinal properties of Norwegian angelica include its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can help soothe digestive issues, reduce pain and inflammation, and combat viral infections. The health benefits of consuming Norwegian angelica include reducing stress and anxiety, promoting digestion, and boosting the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Norwegian angelica can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its roots and leaves can be used fresh or dried to make herbal preparations, such as salves and ointments.
